evidently.experiment.metricgoalobject.Cf: Use this structure to tell Evidently whether higher or lower values are desired for a metric that is used in an experiment.

evidently.experiment.metricgoalobject.En.Description: The entity, such as a user or session, that does an action that causes a metric value to be recorded. An example is `userDetails.userID`.

evidently.experiment.metricgoalobject.Ev.Description: The EventBridge event pattern that defines how the metric is recorded.  For more information about EventBridge event patterns, see [Amazon EventBridge event patterns](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eventbridge/latest/userguide/eb-event-patterns.html).

evidently.experiment.metricgoalobject.Me.Description: A name for the metric. It can include up to 255 characters.

evidently.experiment.metricgoalobject.Un.Description: A label for the units that the metric is measuring.

evidently.experiment.metricgoalobject.Va.Description: The JSON path to reference the numerical metric value in the event.
